NOTICE OF NON OPPOSITION TO MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT (TRANSACTION ID # 66532069) FILED BY DEFENDANT FRUTOS, DANIEL AVALOS AN INDIVIDUAL

LAW & MOTION, DEPT. 302, AS TO THE APR-27-2021 HEARING RE: DEFENDANT DANIEL FRUTOS' MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT, THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANT DANIEL AVALOS FRUTOS' MOTION TO SET ASIDE THE DEFAULT ENTERED AGAINST HIM ON AUGUST 18, 2020 IS DENIED. THE MOTION IS BEYOND THE JURISDICTION OF THE COURT, AS IT WAS FILED MORE THAN SIX MONTHS AFTER THE DEFAULT WAS ENTERED. (CODE CIV. PROC. SEC. 473(B) [APPLICATION "SHALL BE MADE WITHIN A REASONABLE TIME, IN NO CASE EXCEEDING SIX MONTHS, AFTER THE JUDGMENT, DISMISSAL, ORDER, OR PROCEEDING WAS TAKEN"]; MANSON, IVER & YORK V. BLACK (2009) 176 CAL.APP.4TH 36, 42 ["THE SIX-MONTH TIME LIMIT FOR GRANTING STATUTORY RELIEF IS JURISDICTIONAL AND THE COURT MAY NOT CONSIDER A MOTION FOR RELIEF MADE AFTER THAT PERIOD HAS ELAPSED."].) COUNSEL FOR THE PLAINTIFF IS REQUIRED TO PREPARE AND SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER WHICH REPEATS VERBATIM THE COURT'S RULING. JUDGE: ETHAN P. SCHULMAN; CLERK: SEAN KANE; NOT REPORTED (302/EPS)
DISMISSAL OF ENTIRE ACTION OF ALL PARTIES AND ALL CAUSES OF ACTION WITH PREJUDICE ; ALL PARTIES TO BEAR THEIR OWN FEES AND COSTS (TRANSACTION ID # 66859888)
MASTER JURY CALENDAR SET FOR AUG-23-2021 VACATED. DISMISSALS ON FILE. (206)

calendar.matters:
MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Aug-19-2021 Vacated

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Oct-21-2020 Set For Trial On Aug-23-21 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

Defendant Daniel Avalos Frutos Notice Of Motion And Motion To Set Aside Default
Off Calendar Apr-27-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

HEARING RE: DEFENDANT DANIEL FRUTOS' Motion To Set Aside Default.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Defendant Daniel Avalos Frutos' motion to set aside the default entered against him on August 18, 2020 is denied. The motion is beyond the jurisdiction of the court, as it was filed more than six months after the default was entered. (Code Civ. Proc. sec. 473(b) [application "shall be made within a reasonable time, in no case exceeding six months, after the judgment, dismissal, order, or proceeding was taken"]; Manson, Iver & York v. Black (2009) 176 Cal.App.4th 36, 42 ["The six-month time limit for granting statutory relief is jurisdictional and the court may not consider a motion for relief made after that period has elapsed."].)

Counsel for the plaintiff is required to prepare and submit a proposed order which repeats verbatim the Court's ruling.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported (302/EPS)
